window_expansion_note: 72h strict window yielded 5 qualifying items; no expansion required

exploration.non_registry_items_notes: AIR security research and OpenAI Jalapeño were not in source registry; found via exploratory and adversarial passes

published_items.title: Mistral ships OCR 4 with 170-language coverage and self-hosted deployment option
key_claims: 170 languages supported across 10 language groups → mistral.ai/news/ocr-4/ (T2)
OlmOCRBench public leaderboard score: 85.20 (top score) → OlmOCRBench public evaluation (T2)
72% win rate in blind preference tests → Mistral-commissioned annotator evaluation, 600+ documents (T3 — vendor-commissioned)
$4 per 1,000 pages API; $2 Batch API → mistral.ai/news/ocr-4/ (T2)
2,000 pages/min single GPU → mistral.ai/news/ocr-4/ (T2 — vendor-stated, not independently replicated)
Available via Mistral Studio API, Amazon SageMaker, Microsoft Foundry, and self-hosted single container
caveats: 72% win rate is from Mistral's own commissioned evaluation — not an independent third-party benchmark
Throughput claim (2,000 pages/min) not independently reproduced
Self-hosted deployment requires sales contact for enterprise licensing

published_items.title: Claude Code ships /rewind, 37% CPU cut, and bash autocomplete across two back-to-back releases
key_claims: /rewind support — resume conversation from before /clear was run → v2.1.191 (T2)
37% CPU reduction during streaming via text-update coalescing → v2.1.191 (T2)
Background agent resurrection fix — permanent → v2.1.191 (T2)
MCP server reliability improvements via transient-error retries → v2.1.191 (T2)
autoMode.classifyAllShell — routes all Bash/PowerShell through auto-mode classifier → v2.1.193 (T2)
Live file path autocomplete in bash mode → v2.1.193 (T2)
claude_code.assistant_response OpenTelemetry log event → v2.1.193 (T2)

published_items.title: Security firm publishes fake AI agent skill that bypassed Cisco and NVIDIA scanners and spread to 26,000 agents
published_items.primary_url_status: AIR research paper not directly accessed; confirmed via 4 independent trade press outlets
published_items.confirmation_method: The Hacker News, CyberNews, Security Boulevard, The Next Web — all reporting AIR research consistently
key_claims: ~26,000 agents reached → AIR research, via thehackernews.com (T3 — AIR's own measurement)
Bypassed Cisco and NVIDIA skill scanners → AIR research, via thehackernews.com (T3)
Attack vector: mutable external link post-scan → thenextweb.com (T3, corroborating)
caveats: AIR is the author of this finding — security-vendor research, not yet independently replicated
26,000 agent count is AIR's own measurement
Original AIR research paper not directly accessed; details sourced from trade press

published_items.title: OpenAI unveils Jalapeño inference chip, co-built with Broadcom in nine months
published_items.confirmation_method: TechCrunch, CNBC, CNN Business (all T3 secondaries)
key_claims: Nine-month ASIC development cycle from design to tape-out → TechCrunch, CNBC (T3)
Production deployment targeted end 2026 → TechCrunch, CNBC (T3)
'Materially stronger performance-per-watt' vs current alternatives → OpenAI claim via CNBC (T4 — vendor comparative, no methodology)
Pre-training continues on NVIDIA → TechCrunch (T3)
caveats: OpenAI primary source unreachable from research environment (HTTP 403); all details sourced from secondary reporting
No TFLOPS, power draw, memory capacity, or process node disclosed
Performance-per-watt claim is vendor-stated with no methodology
No independent benchmark published at time of announcement

published_items.title: ByteDance launches Doubao Seed 2.1 Pro and Turbo, targeting coding and agent workflows
published_items.tier_nuance: Comparative benchmark claims ('comparable to GPT-5.5') downgraded to T4 — vendor-designed evaluations, not independently replicated
key_claims: Seed 2.1 Pro: 6 yuan/M input tokens, 30 yuan/M output → ByteDance primary (T2)
Seed 2.1 Turbo: 3 yuan/M input, 15 yuan/M output → ByteDance primary (T2)
1-million-token context window → ByteDance primary (T2)
Leads OSWorld, MobileWorld, MMMU-Pro → ByteDance evaluations (T4 — vendor-designed benchmarks)
'Comparable to GPT-5.5' on coding and agent benchmarks → ByteDance claim (T4 — vendor comparative, not independently verified)
caveats: All comparative benchmark scores are from ByteDance's own evaluation suite
No independent third-party evaluation published at time of announcement
China data-residency: compliance review mandatory before production use

limitations_summary: openai.com/index/openai-broadcom-jalapeno-inference-chip/ returned HTTP 403; Jalapeño confirmed via TechCrunch, CNBC, CNN Business (T3 secondaries)
grok.com/release-notes returned HTTP 403; Grok release dates cross-checked via releasebot.io and MarkTechPost
AIR research paper not directly accessed; details sourced from 4 independent trade press outlets
X timelines, LinkedIn private feeds, and Discord not accessed directly
SEA/Indonesia dedicated search pass yielded no publishable in-window items; coverage US/EU/China-heavy
Categories mcp-ecosystem, workflow-automation, productivity-ai, research-papers, policy-regulation had no qualifying in-window items
All Doubao Seed 2.1 benchmark scores are from ByteDance's internal evaluation suite; no independent third-party evaluation published
Mistral OCR 4 72% win rate is vendor-commissioned, not a truly independent evaluation
suggested_follow_ups: Jalapeño independent benchmarks — no TFLOPS/process node/power draw disclosed; watch for MLPerf or independent inference benchmarks H2 2026
ByteDance Doubao Seed 2.1 third-party evaluation — Artificial Analysis or LMSYS Arena benchmarks would confirm or regrade competitive claims
Mistral OCR 4 enterprise adoption — watch for SageMaker and Foundry marketplace integration announcements
AIR agent skill security follow-up — whether Cisco and NVIDIA publish remediation timelines or updated vetting processes
